title: melissa officinalis var. villosa
intro: Melissa officinalis var. villosa, also known as Lemon Balm, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to Europe and the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, which help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Lemon Balm include its ability to soothe stress and promote relaxation, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections and promote wound healing. Lemon Balm can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical cream, and is also used in cooking and as an ingredient in herbal remedies.
